programme.title: The Cook Up with Adam Liaw
programme.desc: TV presenter and writer Myf Warhurst and Attica founder Ben Shewry join Adam to dish up the dinners they like to cook at home.

programme.title: Spartakus and the Sun Beneath the Sea
programme.desc: While exploring a cave, Bob and his sister Rebecca meet Arkana, messenger of the city of Arkadia, nestled in the centre of the Earth.

programme.title: The Mysterious Cities of Gold
programme.desc: The Bedouin chief presents the children with the second veil they were looking for, passed down from generation to generation by the Chaldis tribe. They try to decipher a plan to the city of gold.

programme.title: The Mysterious Cities of Gold
programme.desc: On board the nave, Laguerra prevents Gaspard from killing Mendoza, arguing that Mendoza would be more useful to their plan alive than dead.

programme.title: Little J & Big Cuz
programme.desc: Little J and Big Cuz vow to protect the old grinding stone they find out bush.

programme.title: Motown Magic
programme.sub-title: I Heard It Through The Grapevine and Just My Imagination Running Away With Me
programme.desc: Imaginative boy Ben transforms his city by bringing colourful street art to life, armed with a magic paintbrush and the classic sounds of motown.

programme.title: Harlem Globetrotters: Play It Forward
programme.desc: Bulldog visits his hometown of Corpus Christi where he, Hotshot, Hammer, and Coach Lou challenge Little Brothers and Little Sisters to an unforgettable game of basketball.

programme.title: Garma: Celebrating 25 Years
programme.desc: Stories from Yolngu Country celebrating 25 years of Garma.

programme.title: Te Ao with Moana
programme.desc: A weekly current affairs show that examines New Zealand and international stories through a Maori lens.

programme.title: Bamay
programme.desc: Slow TV is back, celebrating stunning landscapes of Countries across Australia. Sit back and relax with the healing powers of Country.

programme.title: NITV News Update
programme.desc: NITV News recaptures the highlights of Indigenous news around the country.

programme.title: Seasons of the Wild
programme.sub-title: The Everglades: The Season of Drought
programme.desc: In Southern Florida, a unique and complex ecosystem known as the Everglades has entered the winter season. Dry conditions and lower temperatures bring about significant changes to the landscape.

programme.title: NITV Weather
programme.desc: Australian First Nations essential weekend weather forecasts, preparedness tips and cultural knowledge.

programme.title: Going Places With Ernie Dingo
programme.desc: Ernie catches up with pro-surfer and artist Otis Carey, and gets a cultural tour in Coffs Harbour with the Gumbaynggirr mob, Rae learns how our cuppas go from crop-to-cup at a local coffee farm.

programme.title: True Colours
programme.desc: Aboriginal detective Toni Alma is 35 and has her life all worked out. Living in Alice Springs, she's got a great job, a terrific female boss and mentor in Rhonda, who has her marked out for big things in the police force. But something happened two weeks ago to turn all that on its head. Toni broke up with her white boyfriend Nick and carries another secret, she's pregnant and she can't tell anyone, least of all her ex-boyfriend. This throws her life and work into chaos, she doesn't know why she's suddenly acting out, turning vigilante and ignoring the rules.

programme.title: Se7en
programme.desc: Two detectives find themselves following a series of murders; they find clues linking the deaths and the seven sins. The movie follows the cops, their family and tracking down the mass murderer.

programme.title: Words Matter
programme.desc: Mikmaw poet and published author Rebecca Thomas uses words for a living, but she can't speak the language stolen from her father at residential school.

programme.title: The Secrets of Pecan Summer
programme.desc: We reveal the true story behind the first Indigenous opera. Composed by Deborah Cheetham, Pecan Summer is her discovery of what really happened to her family in 1939 and why she never knew the truth.

programme.title: Going Places With Ernie Dingo
programme.desc: K'gari, also known as Fraser Island, is Ernie's next destination. He meets up with a proud Butchulla man, a four wheel drive enthusiast, and a determined woman with a special connection to whales.

programme.title: NITV On the Road: Mbantua
programme.desc: A weekend of culture and music in central Australia.

programme.title: NITV On the Road: Mbantua
programme.desc: A weekend of culture and music in central Australia.

programme.title: NITV On the Road: Mbantua
programme.desc: A weekend of culture and music in central Australia.

programme.title: Bamay
programme.desc: Bamay is back! See more stunning and diverse Australian locations from the Australian skies.

programme.title: Bamay
programme.desc: In Eps 6 and 7, we journey to Adnyamathanha Country, where Wilpena Pound, known to the Traditional Owners as Ikara meaning \.

programme.title: Toi Time
programme.desc: Jojo, Maia and Tama find out about Tamanuitera and Aunty Anika makes kai with vegetables from her mara.

programme.title: Waabiny Time
programme.desc: Do you feel djoorabiny, do you feel happy? Or do you feel menditj, do you feel sick? Make sure you share how you feel with someone who cares. It's moorditj koolangka!

programme.title: Bird's Eye View
programme.desc: After watching a children's Space-themed birthday party the junior birds devise a plan to launch themselves into the great unknown.

programme.title: Aussie Bush Tales
programme.desc: The children are away gathering shellfish but Moort is hungry for quandongs. He goes off to gather some and things don't quite go as planned.

programme.title: The Mysterious Cities of Gold
programme.desc: Using his gift to make the sun reappear, Esteban allows Zia to take off on the Condor. Under the astonished eyes of Yoshi, the immense golden bird finally emerges from the waves.

programme.title: Bushwhacked!
programme.desc: Kamil challenges Kayne to rescue a venomous, temperamental king brown snake and the king brown is not too happy about it.

programme.title: Motown Magic
programme.sub-title: Ain't No Mountain High Enough/For Once in My Life
programme.desc: Imaginative boy Ben transforms his city by bringing colourful street art to life, armed with a magic paintbrush and the classic sounds of motown.

programme.title: The Magic Canoe
programme.desc: Nico has a bad cold and cannot participate in the fun adventure. In the end, he realises that imagination is a wonderful power that he can use whenever he wants.

programme.title: Spartakus and the Sun Beneath the Sea
programme.desc: The slave Tada was charged with carrying the sacred insignia of his office to the King of Benin. If he accomplishes this assignment, he will be finally a man free.

programme.title: The World According to Grandpa
programme.desc: Louie asks 'What are stars?' and Grandpa spins one of his tall tales about Handyman Wilf, who does the strangest odd-jobs for the grateful residents of a big tower block.

programme.title: Chums
programme.desc: Pterry and Ira befriend a blind crow who uses his special hearing to help him fly.

programme.title: Seasons of the Wild
programme.sub-title: The Everglades: The Season of Drought
programme.desc: In Southern Florida, a unique and complex ecosystem known as the Everglades has entered the winter season. Dry conditions and lower temperatures bring about significant changes to the landscape.

programme.title: NITV News Update
programme.desc: NITV News recaptures the highlights of Indigenous news around the country.

programme.title: Going Places With Ernie Dingo
programme.desc: Ernie catches up with pro-surfer and artist Otis Carey, and gets a cultural tour in Coffs Harbour with the Gumbaynggirr mob, Rae learns how our cuppas go from crop-to-cup at a local coffee farm.

programme.title: Storm Boy
programme.desc: Based on the beloved book, Storm Boy is a highly emotional tale of a young boy growing up on the beautiful but uninhabited coastline of Southern Australia who unexpectedly rescues and then raises three orphaned pelicans. When he forms a close bond with them, he finds himself at odds with his fisherman father and his life takes a new and unexpected turn.

programme.title: Road Open
programme.desc: Stories from the Holy Rosary School and community in Derby, Western Australia.

programme.title: Stompem Ground
programme.desc: Stompem Ground 2022 opening act, Country/Rock family band hailing from the Dampier Peninsular/Broome WA.

programme.title: On Country Kitchen
programme.desc: Derek learns how to make beetroot pasta, goes cattle herding on a four-wheeler and even heads country on a real hunt in the bush for indigenous bloodroot with a father and son.

programme.title: The Cook Up with Adam Liaw
programme.desc: Adam, comedian Jennifer Wong and restaurateur Sammy Jakubiak are making some deli delights.

programme.title: Spartakus and the Sun Beneath the Sea
programme.desc: In the city of Arkadia, the children note that life without Shagshag becomes more and more difficult.

programme.title: The Mysterious Cities of Gold
programme.desc: The original 1980s animation classic that follows a young orphan called Esteban, as he searches the new world for both his father and the mysterious Cities of Gold.

programme.title: The Mysterious Cities of Gold
programme.desc: The original 1980s animation classic that follows a young orphan called Esteban, as he searches the new world for both his father and the mysterious Cities of Gold.

programme.title: Little J & Big Cuz
programme.desc: Little J and Big Cuz compete to create the 'best welcome ever' for a surprise guest.

programme.title: Motown Magic
programme.sub-title: Superstition and How Funky is Your Chicken
programme.desc: Imaginative boy Ben transforms his city by bringing colourful street art to life, armed with a magic paintbrush and the classic sounds of motown.

programme.title: Harlem Globetrotters: Play It Forward
programme.desc: Thunder and Hot Rod visit a school in the Dominican Republic that teaches kids the virtues of harvesting their own crops. The team recounts the history of their beloved \.

programme.title: Garma: Celebrating 25 Years
programme.desc: Stories from Yolngu Country celebrating 25 years of Garma.

programme.title: NITV News: Nula
programme.desc: The latest news from the oldest living culture, join Natalie Ahmat and the team of journalists for stories from an Indigenous perspective.

programme.title: Bamay
programme.sub-title: Barkinji - Ngyiampaa - Mutthi Mutthi Country - Mungo NSW
programme.desc: Slow TV is back, celebrating stunning landscapes of Countries across Australia. Sit back and relax with the healing powers of Country.

programme.title: The Mountain of SGaana
programme.desc: The Mountain of SGaana spins a magical tale of a young man who is stolen away to the spirit world and the young woman who rescues him. Haida filmmaker Christopher Auchter's dream-like gem brilliantly.

programme.title: Seasons of the Wild
programme.sub-title: The Everglades: The Season of Flood
programme.desc: In Southern Florida, a unique and complex ecosystem known as the Everglades has entered the summer season. Wet conditions and higher temperatures bring about significant changes to the landscape.

programme.title: NITV Weather
programme.desc: Australian First Nations essential weekend weather forecasts, preparedness tips and cultural knowledge.

programme.title: Princess Mononoke
programme.desc: While seeking to cure himself of a curse, young warrior Ashitaka stumbles into a conflict between the people of Iron Town and Princess Mononoke, a girl raised by wolves, who will stop at nothing to prevent the destruction of her home.

programme.title: First Nation Bedtime Stories
programme.desc: Ivy and Steven visit Pop Len at his home in Perth. He tells them a scary story of the balyit - a small mischievous creature that can come and take little kids if they aren't asleep at night time.

programme.title: The Air Up There
programme.desc: A basketball coach travels to Africa to recruit a new star.

programme.title: NITV News: Nula
programme.desc: The latest news from the oldest living culture, join Natalie Ahmat and the team of journalists for stories from an Indigenous perspective.

programme.title: Bamay
programme.sub-title: Murrumbidgee River - Madi Madi, Dadi Dadi & Nganguruku Country
programme.desc: A slow TV showcase of the stunning landscapes found in Madi Madi, Dadi Dadi and Nganguruku Country along the waters of the Murrumbidgee River.

programme.title: Going Places With Ernie Dingo
programme.desc: Ernie visits the Blue Mountains in NSW to meet up with an enthusiastic cave guide, a Gundungurra man maintaining his connection to Country, and a nature lover who finds solace in this wilderness.

programme.title: NITV on the Road: Saltwater Freshwater
programme.desc: Lawrie shares the stories behind the songs and talks about the history of Coloured Stone when they first started touring and where they are today.

programme.title: NITV on the Road: Saltwater Freshwater
programme.desc: A homegrown band from the mid north coast of NSW, Whitehouse rock it out with their funky grooves, and frontman Grant Saunders shares his personal stories.

programme.title: NITV on the Road: Saltwater Freshwater
programme.desc: Brother and sister duo Joel and Naomi Wenitong share their story about the history of the band, their childhood musical influences and the tragic accident that nearly ended it all.

programme.title: Bamay
programme.desc: We journey to Adnyamathanha Country, where Wilpena Pound, known to the Traditional Owners as Ikara meaning \.

programme.title: Bamay
programme.desc: In this two-part episode, we explore one of Australia's popular destinations, Queensland's Whitsundays, located on Birri Gubba Country. Offering stunning views and a rich cultural history.

programme.title: Toi Time
programme.desc: Maia, Tama and Jojo spot zebras and crocodiles on safari while Aunty Anika finds animals in her room.

programme.title: Waabiny Time
programme.desc: There are maar keny bonar, six seasons. Birak is hot time, time for djiba-djobaliny, swimming time.

programme.title: Bird's Eye View
programme.desc: After obsessively watching TV, Big Jim realises humans can't get enough of talent shows. So why not have one of their own? The best act wins!

programme.title: Aussie Bush Tales
programme.desc: Moort visits the Billabong, hoping to get some nice eggs for breakfast, but there are too many spiders, bugs and snakes.

programme.title: The Mysterious Cities of Gold
programme.desc: Near the temple of Izumo, the children pull Esteban from the water, but attract the attention of one of the guards who chases after them.

programme.title: Bushwhacked!
programme.desc: Kayne and Kamil brave shark-infested waters, dodge saltwater crocodiles and come face-to-face with venomous sea snakes before meeting the box jellyfish.

programme.title: Motown Magic
programme.sub-title: I Heard It Through The Grapevine and Just My Imagination Running Away With Me
programme.desc: Imaginative boy Ben transforms his city by bringing colourful street art to life, armed with a magic paintbrush and the classic sounds of motown.

programme.title: The Magic Canoe
programme.desc: Julie sees Viola hugging Pam and calling her her little treasure. She imagines that her aunt prefers Pam! In this funny adventure, Julie understands that even though Viola shows Pam love, it doesn't take away from the love her aunt has for her.

programme.title: Spartakus and the Sun Beneath the Sea
programme.desc: At the edge of the ice layer of Icelandis there may be a door leading to Arkadia. If you reach it, you have to overcome many dangers, solve many puzzles, and dare to face the terrible Arakoee.

programme.title: The World According to Grandpa
programme.sub-title: What Happened to All the Dinosaurs?
programme.desc: Connie asks 'What happened to the dinosaurs?' and Grandpa spins a tall tale about the world's biggest game of hide-and-seek between all the world's dinosaurs and all the world's mice!

programme.title: Chums
programme.desc: Ira recruits Pterry to make the first ever \.

programme.title: Karla Grant Presents
programme.desc: The moving story of Warri and Yatungka, the last of the Mandildjara people to be living a traditional nomadic lifestyle in the remote Gibson Desert of central Australia.

programme.title: Stompem Ground
programme.desc: A five-piece band line up from Broome, Footprince.

programme.title: Hairspray
programme.desc: It's Baltimore, 1962, and rebellious teenager Tracy Turnblad attempts to win the coveted Miss Auto Show crown as she fights against racial discrimination, in this film created and directed by John Waters, and featuring rock legends Sonny Bono and Deborah Harry.

programme.title: NITV News: Nula
programme.desc: The latest news from the oldest living culture, join Natalie Ahmat and the team of journalists for stories from an Indigenous perspective.

programme.title: Seasons of the Wild
programme.sub-title: The Everglades: The Season of Flood
programme.desc: In Southern Florida, a unique and complex ecosystem known as the Everglades has entered the summer season. Wet conditions and higher temperatures bring about significant changes to the landscape.

programme.title: Over the Black Dot
programme.desc: In 2026, Over the Black Dot becomes a digital-first Rugby League yarn free-flowing conversations on footy, culture and community, with panellists and guests sharing authentic Indigenous stories.

programme.title: Singing Back The Buffalo
programme.desc: Richly visualised and deeply uplifting, Singing Back the Buffalo is an epic reimagining of North America through the lens of buffalo consciousness and a potent dream of what is within our grasp.
